# Priority research queue

This is the order to run the expanded hypotheses. Do not start with niche-specific polish. Start with the gates that decide whether the video gets any chance to perform.

## Phase 1 — Stop-scroll and classification

Run first:

- First-frame variants: result, problem, proof, face, before/after.
- Hook variants: problem-first, result-first, proof-first, identity, direct-search.
- Topic alignment: same exact phrase across speech, text, caption and title.

Primary metrics:

```text
YouTube Shorts: chose-to-view, engaged views, avg % viewed
TikTok: 1–3 sec hold, full watch, search traffic
Instagram Reels: average watch time, non-follower reach, sends/saves
```

## Phase 2 — Comprehension and retention

Run second:

- Captions: auto vs edited vs cleaned sentence captions.
- Text overlays: short headline vs full central overlay vs labels vs annotations.
- Visual rhythm: decorative cuts vs proof/meaning cuts.
- Time-to-first-payoff: 2s vs 5s vs 10s vs end.

Primary metrics:

```text
avg watch time
avg percentage viewed
completion
rewatch
readability complaints
```

## Phase 3 — Save/send/comment/follow design

Run third:

- Saveable checklist vs general tip.
- Send-to-specific-person CTA vs generic share CTA.
- A/B comment prompt vs open question.
- Standalone tip vs named series.

Primary metrics:

```text
saves per reach
sends per reach
comments per reach
follows per 1,000 views
profile clicks
```

## Phase 4 — Form-specific scaling

Only after the above:

- Talking head vs face + proof.
- Screen recording zoom/annotation tests.
- Podcast clip cold-open tests.
- Product demo problem-first tests.
- Storytime stakes-first tests.
- Livestream clip context-compression tests.

## Phase 5 — Platform-native adaptation

Test the same thesis as different assets:

```text
TikTok: culture/search/comment loop
Reels: send/save/non-follower test
Shorts: first-frame/search/subscriber conversion
LinkedIn: professional tension/proof
Pinterest: evergreen/saveability
X: quotable claim + receipts
```

## Phase 6 — Mechanism scaling

Scale a mechanism only if it wins on at least two of these:

```text
retention
completion
saves/sends
follows/subscribers
7d or 30d durability
positive/neutral trust signal
```
